sample_size.source_quote: a total of 146 patients were included in this retrospective study

patient_age_mean.source_quote: The median age of patients was 2.7 months (interquartile range: 1.0, 10.9 months, Table 1)

icu_mortality_percent.source_quote: A total of 33 (22.6%) patients died within 90 days after the onset of Kp-BSI

mortality_predictors.timing: Within 24 h after the index blood culture
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The pediatric SOFA score had a moderate predictive power for 90-day mortality, with an AUC of 0.80 (95% CI 0.71–0.89), along with a high sensitivity (0.84) and a low specificity (0.68).

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: Kp-specific SOFA (SOFA + prior hospitalization + intra-abdominal source)
mortality_predictors.timing: Within 24 h after the index blood culture
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Receiver operating characteristic curve analysis; DeLong's test; integrated discrimination improvement index
mortality_predictors.cutoff_value: Low risk: 0–8; Medium risk: 9–11; High risk: ≥12
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, admission year, admission ward
mortality_predictors.source_quote: A modified SOFA model combining SOFA plus prior hospitalization and intra-abdominal source of infection was selected as the optimal multivariate predictive model (AUC = 0.89, 95% CI 0.82–0.96; sensitivity = 0.86; specificity = 0.84; positive predictive value = 0.64; negative predictive value = 0.95)

mortality_predictors.timing: Within 24 h after the index blood culture
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, admission year, admission ward
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Among the various types of organ failure, altered mental status was associated with the worst outcome (HR = 5.19, 95% CI 2.50–10.77)

mortality_predictors.timing: Within 24 h after the index blood culture
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, admission year, admission ward

mortality_predictors.effect_size: HR = 6.8-fold higher compared to primary bacteremia or catheter-related infections
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age, sex, admission year, admission ward
mortality_predictors.source_quote: For patients infected with K. pneumoniae from intra-abdominal sources, the risk of death was 6.8-fold higher compared to that of patients with primary bacteremia or catheter-related infections.
